item.title: SG Finserve Ltd Opens 7.96% Higher in Sharp Gap Up, But Can the Technicals Support It?
item.description: SG Finserve Ltd witnessed a robust start to trading on 12 May 2026, opening with a significant gap up of 7.96%, signalling positive momentum in the Non Banking Financial Company (NBFC) sector despite broader sectoral weakness. This surge reflects a notable shift in market sentiment following recent rating upgrades and technical indicators. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Keystone Realtors Q4 FY26: Stellar Quarter Masks Underlying Concerns as Stock Languishes
item.description: Keystone Realtors Ltd., trading under the ticker RUSTOMJEE, delivered a blockbuster fourth quarter for FY26 with consolidated net profit surging to ₹52.42 crores, marking an extraordinary 1,450.89% quarter-on-quarter growth. However, this impressive quarter-on-quarter performance masks a concerning 19.10% year-on-year decline and follows three consecutive quarters of subdued performance. The stock, with a market capitalisation of ₹5,279 crores, has responded tepidly to the results, trading at ₹414.30 as of May 12, 2026, down 2.51% from the previous close and languishing 40.56% below its 52-week high of ₹697. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Ethos Ltd Q4 FY26: Luxury Watch Retailer's Profit Slides Despite Strong Revenue Growth
item.description: Ethos Limited, India's leading luxury watch retailer, reported a consolidated net profit of ₹22.00 crores for Q4 FY26, marking a sharp 27.70% decline quarter-on-quarter and a modest 3.30% drop year-on-year. The results present a concerning picture for the ₹6,210 crore market capitalisation company, as profitability deteriorated despite robust revenue expansion. The stock has been under pressure, trading at ₹2,359.80, down 20.51% year-to-date and 27.27% below its 52-week high of ₹3,244.45. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: A-1 Ltd Q4 FY26: Stellar Quarter Masks Deeper Quality Concerns
item.description: A-1 Limited, a micro-cap speciality chemicals manufacturer with a market capitalisation of ₹441.00 crores, delivered an exceptional fourth quarter performance for FY26, with consolidated net profit surging 354.17% quarter-on-quarter to ₹4.36 crores. However, this impressive headline number conceals persistent structural challenges that continue to weigh on the company's long-term investment appeal. Trading at ₹10.26 on May 12, 2026, the stock has gained 4.91% in the session following the results announcement, though it remains significantly below its 52-week high of ₹41.25. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Worth Peripherals Q4 FY26: Profit Decline Amid Rising Tax Burden Clouds Growth Story
item.description: Worth Peripherals Ltd., an Indore-based packaging solutions provider with a market capitalisation of ₹221.00 crores, reported a consolidated net profit of ₹3.45 crores for the quarter ended March 2026, marking a significant 30.86% decline year-on-year from ₹4.99 crores in the corresponding quarter last year. Despite posting a modest 6.98% revenue growth to ₹76.13 crores, the company's bottom line faced pressure from an elevated tax rate that surged to 35.46%, raising concerns about profitability sustainability in an otherwise stable operational environment. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Avadh Sugar Q4 FY26: Strong Quarter Masks Underlying Volatility Concerns
item.description: Avadh Sugar & Energy Ltd., part of the prestigious K.K. Birla Group, reported a robust fourth quarter for FY26, with net profit surging to ₹55.61 crores, marking a sequential increase of 232.99% from Q3 FY26's ₹16.70 crores. However, the year-on-year comparison reveals a more sobering picture, with profits declining 22.43% from ₹71.68 crores in Q4 FY25. The micro-cap sugar producer, currently trading at ₹497.50 with a market capitalisation of ₹1,005 crores, faces a challenging investment landscape characterised by seasonal volatility and declining institutional confidence. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Saurashtra Cement Q4 FY26: Profitability Recovery Masks Deeper Structural Concerns
item.description: Saurashtra Cement Ltd., the Gujarat-based cement manufacturer with a market capitalisation of ₹671.00 crores, reported a return to profitability in Q4 FY26 after two consecutive quarters of losses, posting a net profit of ₹18.83 crores. However, this represents a sharp 42.78% year-on-year decline from ₹32.91 crores in Q4 FY25, underscoring persistent operational headwinds that continue to pressure the company's earnings power despite sequential improvement. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Madras Fertilizers Q4 FY26: Strong Profit Rebound Masks Operational Fragility
item.description: Madras Fertilizers Ltd., a government-owned fertiliser manufacturer, reported a dramatic turnaround in Q4 FY26, posting a net profit of ₹20.89 crores compared to a loss of ₹52.44 crores in Q4 FY25. The 67.00% quarter-on-quarter revenue surge to ₹699.21 crores drove the recovery, yet the company's shares remain under pressure, trading at ₹71.59 with a market capitalisation of ₹1,081 crores. Despite this quarterly bounce, the stock has declined 18.20% over the past year, significantly underperforming both the Sensex (-9.55%) and the broader fertiliser sector (-12.34%). Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Bright Brothers Q4 FY26: Profit Recovery Masks Persistent Margin Concerns
item.description: Bright Brothers Ltd., a micro-cap manufacturer of injection moulded plastic products with a market capitalisation of ₹158.00 crores, reported a net profit of ₹2.12 crores for Q4 FY26, marking a return to profitability after the preceding quarter's loss of ₹1.38 crores. However, the 15.22% year-on-year growth in net profit fails to mask underlying operational challenges, with margins remaining under pressure and the stock declining 6.58% following the results announcement. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Sarla Performance Fibers Q4 FY26: Massive Loss Overshadows Revenue Recovery
item.description: Sarla Performance Fibers Ltd., a micro-cap player in the garments and apparels sector, reported a devastating fourth quarter for FY26, posting a consolidated net loss of ₹59.64 crores compared to a profit of ₹12.69 crores in the corresponding quarter last year—a staggering decline of 569.98%. Despite a modest 2.19% year-on-year revenue growth to ₹102.53 crores, the company's profitability collapsed due to exceptional items and operational pressures, sending alarm bells through investor circles. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Indian Hotels Q4 FY26: Margin Compression Overshadows Revenue Growth Amid Seasonal Weakness
item.description: Indian Hotels Company Limited, India's largest hospitality chain with a market capitalisation of ₹92,815 crores, reported consolidated net profit of ₹599.86 crores for Q4 FY26, marking a sharp 33.59% quarter-on-quarter decline from ₹903.23 crores in Q3 FY26. Despite delivering year-on-year growth of 14.85%, the results disappointed investors, with the stock plunging 4.03% to ₹634.30 on May 12, 2026, reflecting concerns over significant margin compression and sequential weakness. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Standard Industries Q4 FY25: Losses Deepen as Property Division Struggles Continue
item.description: Standard Industries Ltd. (NSE: SIL), the Mumbai-based property company, reported a net loss of ₹7.21 crores for the quarter ended March 2026, marking a 62.02% deterioration year-on-year from the ₹4.45 crores loss in Q4 FY24. The sequential comparison proved equally concerning, with losses widening 56.06% from the previous quarter's ₹4.62 crores deficit. The stock closed at ₹15.94 on May 12, 2026, down 27.48% from its 52-week high of ₹21.98, reflecting persistent investor scepticism about the company's ability to return to profitability. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Seshasayee Paper Q4 FY26: Margin Pressures Mount Despite Revenue Surge
item.description: Seshasayee Paper & Boards Ltd., a Tamil Nadu-based paper manufacturer with a market capitalisation of ₹1,664 crores, reported consolidated net profit of ₹26.05 crores for Q4 FY26, marking a robust sequential growth of 39.75% from ₹18.64 crores in Q3 FY26. However, the year-on-year comparison reveals a marginal decline of 3.77% from ₹27.07 crores in Q4 FY25, raising concerns about sustained profitability momentum. The stock reacted negatively, falling 5.48% to ₹251.05 in the trading session following the results, reflecting investor apprehension over deteriorating operational margins despite strong topline growth. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Borosil Renewables Q4 FY25: Strong Turnaround with Record Profitability
item.description: Borosil Renewables Ltd., India's second-largest company in the industrial products sector, has delivered a remarkable turnaround in Q4 FY25 (January-March 2025), posting a consolidated net profit of ₹169.14 crores compared to a loss of ₹20.10 crores in the same quarter last year—a dramatic swing that marks the company's strongest quarterly performance on record. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Novartis India Q4 FY26: Profit Slump and Margin Pressures Cloud Strong Revenue Growth
item.description: Novartis India Ltd., the Indian subsidiary of Swiss pharmaceutical giant Novartis AG, reported a concerning quarter-on-quarter decline in profitability for Q4 FY26 despite posting its highest quarterly revenue. Net profit for the March 2026 quarter stood at ₹25.25 crores, marking a sharp 13.80% decline from the previous quarter's ₹29.30 crores, even as revenue climbed 7.95% year-on-year to ₹90.55 crores. The company's stock surged 6.43% in Monday's trading session to ₹1,259.05, bringing its market capitalisation to ₹3,089 crores, as investors appeared to focus on the revenue milestone whilst overlooking the profit deterioration and margin compression that characterised the quarter. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Vinati Organics Q4 FY26: Margin Pressures Persist Despite Profit Recovery
item.description: Vinati Organics Ltd., a leading manufacturer of specialty chemicals and organic intermediaries, reported a marginal improvement in net profit for Q4 FY26, posting ₹123.86 crores compared to ₹123.04 crores in the corresponding quarter last year—a modest year-on-year growth of just 0.67%. However, the quarter-on-quarter performance showed stronger momentum with profits surging 22.84% from ₹100.83 crores in Q3 FY26, driven primarily by improved revenue realisation and operational efficiency gains. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: V-Guard Industries Q4 FY26: Strong Quarter Masks Broader Concerns
item.description: V-Guard Industries Ltd., the Kochi-based electronics and appliances manufacturer, reported a stellar fourth quarter performance for FY26, with net profit surging 96.51% quarter-on-quarter to ₹112.14 crores. However, the impressive quarterly numbers belie deeper concerns about valuation, technical weakness, and year-on-year momentum that have pushed the stock into bearish territory, trading at ₹321.15 as of May 12, 2026—down 22.21% from its 52-week high of ₹412.85. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Kamat Hotels Q4 FY26: Profitability Rebounds Amid Seasonal Headwinds
item.description: Kamat Hotels (India) Ltd., operator of the five-star Orchid Hotel near Mumbai's domestic airport, reported a consolidated net profit of ₹16.39 crores for Q4 FY26, marking a 49.14% year-on-year improvement despite a 5.26% sequential decline from the previous quarter. The Mumbai-based hospitality company, with a market capitalisation of ₹508.00 crores, delivered these results against a backdrop of seasonal revenue softness, as net sales declined 6.47% quarter-on-quarter to ₹110.12 crores. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: NIIT Learning Systems Q4 FY26: Growth Momentum Stalls as Margins Contract Sharply
item.description: NIIT Learning Systems Ltd. reported a mixed performance in Q4 FY26, with net profit rising 58.27% year-on-year to ₹77.11 crores but revealing underlying operational challenges that have spooked investors. The stock plummeted 9.54% following the results announcement, closing at ₹272.50 and erasing significant market value from the ₹4,072 crore company. Whilst absolute profit figures appear robust, a deeper examination reveals concerning margin compression and deteriorating operational efficiency that overshadow the headline growth. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: {{list.post_title}}
item.description: {{list.post_excerpt}} Time: {{list.post_date_sep}}
